# UC-22.46.4 · Tabletop rehearsal evidence — IR plan exercise frequency

## Description

Tracks IR playbook rehearsal frequency per playbook, against per-playbook cadence. Tabletop exercises are the single most-cited IR control in supervisory reviews.

## Value

Shifts IR readiness evidence from 'we rehearse' to 'here is the per-playbook cadence record'.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain ir_playbooks.csv with cadence; (2) emit rehearsal completion records; (3) run monthly.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: IR playbook register, rehearsal signed attestations..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain ir_playbooks.csv with cadence; (2) emit rehearsal completion records; (3) run monthly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup ir_playbooks.csv
| lookup ir_rehearsals.csv playbook_id OUTPUT last_rehearsal signed_by
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_rehearsal,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_rehearsal) OR days_since>cadence_days
| table playbook_id owner last_rehearsal days_since cadence_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Tabletop rehearsal evidence — IR plan exercise frequency** — Shifts IR readiness evidence from 'we rehearse' to 'here is the per-playbook cadence record'.

Documented **Data sources**: IR playbook register, rehearsal signed attestations. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(last_rehearsal) OR days_since>cadence_days` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: cadence_days, days_since, last_rehearsal, owner, playbook_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap of playbook × days-since-rehearsal, single-value '% of playbooks within cadence'.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup ir_playbooks.csv
| lookup ir_rehearsals.csv playbook_id OUTPUT last_rehearsal signed_by
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_rehearsal,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_rehearsal) OR days_since>cadence_days
| table playbook_id owner last_rehearsal days_since cadence_days
```

## Visualization

Heatmap of playbook × days-since-rehearsal, single-value '% of playbooks within cadence'. Key fields: `cadence_days`, `days_since`, `last_rehearsal`, `owner`, `playbook_id`.
